title: Legal AI Bias Coverage Lab
purpose: Profile of the Evidence Bundle spec scoped to pre-deployment + ongoing-monitoring bias / equity-coverage evidence for AI tools used in the practice of law. Covers AI-assisted jury selection (Batson framework), AI-assisted sentencing recommendations (post-ProPublica COMPAS cautionary), AI immigration case triage, AI public defender caseload assignment, AI eDiscovery predictive coding (TAR), AI civil rights case screening, AI contract review for discrimination clauses, and AI client intake. Anchored to: Title VI (federal financial assistance) + Title VII (employment) + Fourteenth Amendment Equal Protection + Sixth Amendment effective assistance + Batson v. Kentucky + ABA Criminal Justice Standards + state bar disparate-impact frameworks. NOT a state-bar attestation. NOT a substitute for outside-counsel civil-rights review.

indigent_defendant_status.source: Sixth Amendment (Gideon v. Wainwright) + state public-defender eligibility

additional_dimensions.rationale: Geographic disparity in AI tool deployment across federal districts + state courts
Pro bono / contingency / hourly / public-defender — affects which AI tools attorneys can afford to use
Title VI LEP — TAR predictive coding accuracy on non-English documents
AI tool access disparity — pro se litigants typically lack tool access
Sentencing-AI recommendation distribution across outcome paths
AI tool accuracy varies — appellate work demands citation-level precision
Procedural-fairness measure — Title VI + state court LEP plan

coverage_status_taxonomy.description: n >= 30 AND gap <= threshold AND four-fifths ratio >= 0.80
n adequate but gap > threshold — trigger ABA Rule 3.3 + supervising-attorney review

Selection-rate ratio < 0.80; trigger civil-rights review pathway; ALL ai-sentencing-recommendation + ai-pretrial-detention-risk-assessment outputs to this subgroup MUST go through supervising-attorney sign-off until cleared
Pattern matches ProPublica's 2016 COMPAS analysis (higher false-positive rate for one subgroup AND higher false-negative rate for another) — model-card disclosure REQUIRED to client + tribunal

AI-assisted peremptory-strike recommendations show statistically significant subgroup-disparate pattern — Batson v. Kentucky framework triggered; attorney MUST review and document a non-discriminatory basis BEFORE acting on the recommendation
LEP-disclosed clients' AI-tool accuracy or accommodation grant rate materially below comparator — Title VI pathway not working
Public-defender-assigned clients receive systematically lower-quality AI recommendations vs retained-counsel clients — Sixth Amendment effective-assistance + state public-defender equity issue
Finding from DOJ Civil Rights Section, state AG civil rights division, state bar disciplinary counsel, or court civil rights office pending

non_obligations_explicitly: This profile does NOT cover non-AI bias in the practice of law (that's a separate civil-rights body of work).
This profile does NOT establish per-state hiring obligations for law firms (that's HR Tech, see candidate-data-vault-contract-profile).
This profile does NOT cover judicial AI use (that binds judges, not attorneys).
